methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Neko Health is a Series B-stage biotech and health company founded by Daniel Ek and Hjalmar Nilsonne, currently valued at $1.8B post-money following $326M+ in total capital raised. The valuation assessment triangulates against a peer set of 8 private companies at the same stage and 3 public-market comparables in the Biotech & Health sector.

Two methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $1.8B base, implying a $3.4B valuation. Peer-median anchoring across four same-stage, same-sector comparables—Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M)—yields a median of $805M, with a range spanning $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp P/S anchoring cannot be applied due to the absence of disclosed revenue for the target.

Triangulation across the two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $684M (low) to $3.9B (high), with a median of $2.1B. The wide dispersion reflects material variance between peer-median and stage-progression methodologies. The current $1.8B valuation sits near the lower-middle of this range, suggesting upside potential, though the gap between peer-median ($805M) and the target's current valuation warrants scrutiny of whether Neko Health commands a meaningful premium relative to its peer set. Key uncertainties include revenue visibility and the comparability of peer capital efficiency.
